description: Diagnose and correct regression misspecification — heteroskedasticity, serial correlation, and multicollinearity — using Breusch-Pagan, Durbin-Watson/Breusch-Godfrey, and VIF

specification_errors.omitted_variables: Relevant Xs missing — biases remaining coefficients
specification_errors.inappropriate_form_of_variables: Use levels vs first differences inappropriately

heteroskedasticity.consequences: Coefficient estimates remain unbiased but SEs are wrong; t-tests unreliable
heteroskedasticity.detection: Breusch-Pagan test; Chi-sq with k df under H0 of homoskedasticity
heteroskedasticity.correction: White (robust) standard errors; weighted least squares

serial_correlation.consequences: SEs underestimated for positive autocorrelation; inflated t-stats
serial_correlation.detection_dw: Durbin-Watson ≈ 2(1-r); compare to dl/du critical values
serial_correlation.detection_bg: Breusch-Godfrey LM test for higher-order autocorrelation
serial_correlation.correction: Newey-West HAC standard errors; first-difference or AR(1) transformation

multicollinearity.consequences: SEs inflate; individual t-stats become insignificant despite high R²
multicollinearity.detection_vif: Variance Inflation Factor = 1/(1-R²_j); VIF > 10 suggests problematic collinearity
multicollinearity.correction: Drop one of correlated Xs; use principal components; collect more data

errors.message: violation_type must be heteroskedasticity, serial_correlation, multicollinearity, or omitted_variable

anti_patterns.rule: Do not rely on Durbin-Watson when lagged dependent variables are regressors
anti_patterns.why: DW is biased toward 2 in such models; use Breusch-Godfrey LM test instead

anti_patterns.rule: Do not interpret high R² as evidence of correct specification
anti_patterns.why: Multicollinearity and omitted-variable bias both leave R² high while individual coefficient inferences are misleading

anti_patterns.rule: Do not drop multicollinear variables without examining theory
anti_patterns.why: Mechanical variable elimination can remove economically essential regressors; collinearity may be tolerable if forecasting and not testing individual betas
